244-19  Octob er 29, 2019  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Montclair   Golf   Club   ( “ the   Club ” ),   located   at   25   Prospect   Avenue ,   West  Orange,   N ew   J ersey   07052,   has   arranged   for   a   small   helicopter   to   land   on   the   grounds   of   the  Club   to   deliver   Santa   Claus   to   Club   members   and   thei r   guests   in   att endance   for   a   holiday  celebration;   and  WHERAS,   this   year ’ s   annual   Santa   Claus   event   is   scheduled   for   Sunday ,   December   8 ,  201 9   at approximately   11:00   am ; and  WHERAS,   Santa ’ s   reindeer,   Rudolph   et   als.,   are   not   available   for   the   trip   at   this   time   of  year as   they must be well-rested in preparation for their big Christmas Eve event; and  WHERAS,   Santa   Claus   has   again   graciously   managed   to   make   arrangements   to   travel   by  small helicopter to attend the Club ’ s annual holiday celebration; and  WHERAS,   the   New   Jers ey   Department   of   Transportatio n,   Division   of   Aeronautics ,  Inspection   &   Aircraft   Operation,   not   wanting   to   be   Scrooge-like,   does   permit   and   has   in   the   past  permitted   helicopter   landings   such   as   these   on   country   club   property   when   approv ed   pursuant   to  its ap plication process; and  WHERAS,   Dennis   Le a ver   ,   the   helicopter   pilot   and   agent   for   the   Club,   will   prepare   the  required   Application   for   Temporary   Aeronautical   Facility   License   for   submission   to   the   New  Jersey   Department   of   Transportation,   and   whi ch   must   be   accompanied   by,   among   other  documents   and   things   which   the   Club   will   provide,   a   “ letter,   statement,   or   certificate   from   the  appropriate   governing   body   which   states   there   is   no   objection   to   the   issuance   of   a   temporary  license (mandatory for all t emps) ” ; and

4846-7964-8168, v. 1  WHERAS,   for   this   event,   the   appropriate   body   is   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township  of West Orange; and  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   that   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange ,   does   and   hereby   states   that   it   has   no   objection   t o   the   issua nce   of   a   Temporary  Aeronautical   Facility   License   to   the   Montclair   Golf   Club ,   located   at   25   Prospect   Avenue ,   West  Orange,   NJ   07052,   for   the   purpose   of   permitting   transportation   and   delivery   of   Santa   Claus   to   the  Club   at   approximate ly   11:00am .,   on   Sunday ,   De cember   8 ,   201 9 ,   for   participation   in   the   Club ’ s  tr aditional Christmas celebration; and it is further  RESOLVED,   that   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office   for  reasonable inspection in accordance with applic able law.  Karen J. Carnevale , R.M.C.   Jerry Guarino  Municipal   Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   October 29, 2019

2 45 -19  October   29 , 2019  RESOLU TION  Whereas ,   the State of New Jersey, Department of Environmental Protection  awarded the Township with Green Acres Project #0722-035, West Orange  Township Multi-Park Development Project, Lafayette Park, Block 61, Lot 27; and  Whereas , subsequent to this award, the Township acquired the Rock Spring Golf  Club with unrestricted funding, with the intention to ultimately preserve the  majority of the pro perty for recreational use and utilize a minor portion for other  development us es; and  Whereas , the Township has not accepted funding from Green Acres Project  #0722-035; and  Whereas ,   acceptance of the funding from Green Acres Project #0722-035 would  re quire the Township list the Rock Spring Golf Club in its entirety on the  townshi p ’ s Recreation and Open Space inventory; and  Whereas,   such a listing on the Recreation and Open Space inventory would  automatically dedicate all of the gold course parcels to   recreation or open space  uses, eliminating the possibility to use a minor portio n for other development uses  in the future;  Now   Therefore   Be It Resolved , that the West Orange Township Council does  officially withdraw Green Acres Project #0722-035 and any ot her open projects at  this time.  Be It Further Resolved , that a certified copy   of this resolution be forwarded to the  State of New Jersey, Department of Environmental Protection, Green Acres  Program.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Jerry Guarino, Council President  Municipal Clerk  Adopte d: October   29 , 2019

2 4 6-19  October 29, 2019  WHEREAS , land use is an essential component of overall sustainability for a municipality; and  WHEREAS , poor land-use decisions can lead to and increase   societal ills such as decreased mobility,  high housing costs, increased greenhouse gas emissions, loss of open space and the degradation of natural  resources; and  WHEREAS , well planned land use can create transportation choices, provide for a range of hous ing  options, create walkable communities, preserve open space, provide for adequate recreation, and allow for  the continued protection and use of vital natural resources; and  WHEREAS , Given New Jersey ’ s strong tradition of home rule and local authority ove r planning and  zoning, achieving a statewide sustainable land use pattern will require municipalities to take the lead;  NOW THEREFORE , we the   Town ship of West Orange , resolve to take the following steps with regard  to our municipal land-use decisions with the i ntent of making   West Orange   a truly sustainable  community. It is our intent to include these principles in the next master plan revision and reexamination  report and to update our land-use zoning, natural resource protection, and other ordina nces accordingly.  Regional Cooperation   - We pledge to reach out to administrations of our neighboring municipalities  concerning land-use decisions, and to take into consideration regional impacts when making land-use  decisions.  Transportation Choice s - We   pledge to create transportation choices with a Complete Streets approach  by considering all modes of transportation, including walking, biking, transit and automobiles, when  planning transportation projects and reviewing development applications. We will r eevaluate our parking  with the goal of limiting the amount of required parking spaces, promoting shared parking and other  innovative parking alternatives, and encouraging structured parking alternatives where appropriate.  Natural Resource Protection   - We p ledge to take action to protect the natural resources of the State for  environmental, recreational and agricultural value, avoiding or mitigating negative impacts to these  resources. Further, we pledge to complete a Natural Resources Inventory when feasibl e to identify and  assess the extent of our natural resources and to link natural resource management and protection to  carrying capacity analysis, land-use and open space planning.  Mix of Land Use s - We pledge to use our zoning power to allow for a mix of   residential, retail,  commercial, recreational and other land use types in areas that make the most sense for our municipality  and the region, particularly in downtown and town center areas.

Housing Options   - We pledge, through the use of our zoning and rev enue generating powers, to foster a  diverse mix of housing types and locations, including single- and multi-family, for-sale and rental  options, to meet the needs of all people at a range of income levels.  Green Design   - We pledge to incorporate the princi ples of green design and renewable energy generation  into municipal buildings to the extent feasible and when updating our site plan and subdivision  requirements for residential and commercial buildings.  Municipal Facilities Siting   - We pledge, to the exte nt feasible, to take into consideration factors such as  walkability, bikability, greater access to public transit, proximity to other land-use types, and open space  when locating new or relocated municipal facilities.  Adopted: October 29, 2019  CERT IFICATION  I hereby certify this is a true and exact copy of a resolution adopted by the   West Orange Township  Council   on   10/29/2019  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M .C., Munici pal Clerk

pages.text: 247-19  October 29, 2019  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Police   Department   (the   “ WOPD ” )   seeks   to  utilize   the   property   owned   by   the   Township   of   Livingston ,   New   Jersey   (the   “ Livingston  Facility ” ) for   firearms training activities   (the   “ Training ” ) for officers   of the   WOPD ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   Livingston   requires   the   execution   of   a   Release   and   Hold  Harmless   Agreement   (the   “ Release ” ),   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A, ”   for   use   of   the   Livingston  Facility for the   Training ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   Livingston   is   allowing   the   WOPD   to   utilize   the   Livingston  Facility for the   Training   at no cost to the Township of West Orange.  NOW   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF  THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Township   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to  execut e the Release annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A; ”   and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Police   Chief   for   the   Township   of   West   Orange   i s   authorized   to  execute   the Release on behalf of the Township; and   be it further  RESOLVED,   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office  in accordance with applicable law.  Karen   J.   Carnevale , R.M.C.   Jerry Guarino  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   October 29, 2019

247-19  RELEASE   AND   HOLD   HARMLESS   AGREEMENT  In   consideration   for   license   to   enter   upon   and   use   the   property,   including   buildings,  facilities,   pool   and   grounds   owned   by   the Township of West Orange   (the   “   Premises ” )   o n  ( N o v e m b e r   1 1 - N o v e m b e r   2 2 ,   2 0 1 9   )   for   Firearms   training   activities   (the  “ Training ” )   conducted   by   West   Orange   Police   Dep ar t ment   (the   “ WOPD ” ) ,   the   Township   of   West  Orange (the   “ Township ” ) :  1.   Hereby   releases,   waives,   discharges   and   covenants   not   to   sue   the   Township   of   Livingston   for  any   and   all   damage   and   any   claim   or   demands   therefore   arising   from   the   use   of   the   Premises  by   members   of   the   WOPD   for   the   Training   arising   from   or   out   of   the   negligence   of   WOPD,  its   representatives,   agents   and   employees,   related   to   the   use   of   the   Premises   by   WOPD   for  the   Training,   subject   to   the   provisions   of   the   New   Jersey   Tort   Claims   Act   (N.J.S.A.   59:   1-1   et  seq.   and   the   New   Jersey   Contractual   Liability   Act   (N.J.S.A.   59:13-1   et   seq.).  2.   Hereby   agrees   to   hold   harmless   the   Township   of   Livingston   from   and   against   any   and   all  claims,   actions,   damages,   liability   and   expense   in   connection   with   loss   of   life,   personal  injury,   damage   to   property   or   any   other   claim   asserted   whatsoever,   arising   from   or   out   of   the  negligence   of   WOPD,   its   representatives,   agents   and   employees,   related   to   the   use   of   the  Premises   by   WOPD   for   the   Training,   subject   to   the   provisions   of   the   New   Jersey   Tort   Claims  Act   (N.J.S.A.   59:   1-1   et   seq.   and   the   New   Jersey   Contractual   Liability   Act   (N.J.S.A.   59:13-1  et   seq.).  3.   Hereby   assumes   full   and   sole   responsibility   for   compliance   with   any   and   all   local,   state   or  federal   laws,   ordinances,   regulations   or   rules   applicable   to   the   use   of   the   Premises   for   the  purposes   described   herein.  The   undersigned   represents   and   warrants   that   he   or   she   has   full   authority   to   enter   into   this  agreement   has   entered   into   this   agreement   voluntarily   after   reading   and   understanding   it,   and   that  no   other   oral   representations,   statements   or   inducements   apart   from   the   foregoing   written  agreement   have   been   made.  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  WEST ORANGE POLICE DEPARTMENT  By:   Date:  James Abbott, P olice   C hief

248-19  Oc tober 29, 2019  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Essex   County   Division   of   Senior   Services   provides   a   grant   for   the  partial funding of a S enior Citizen Public Health   Visiting   Nurse   (the   “ Grant ” ); and  WHEREAS,   the   Grant   provides   twenty-five   percent   (25%)   of   the   costs,   or   $16,127,   for  the   S enior   Citizen   Public   Health   Visiting   Nurse   for   the   period   of   January   1,   2020   through  December 31, 2020; and  WHEREAS,   the   Essex   County   Division   of   Senior   Services   requires   the   execution   of   an  Agre ement for the award of the Grant, annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A ”   (the   “ Agreement ” ); and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   has   previously   participated   in   this   grant   provided   by   the  Essex County Division of Senior Services; and  WHEREAS,   the   Health   Officer   has   reviewed   th e   Agreement   and   recommends   its  approval and   execution; and  WHEREAS,   the   Health   Officer   has   reviewed   the   annexed   agreement   and   recommends  execution and approval of the contract .  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   that   the   the   Mayor   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   the  Agreement, annexed   hereto as Exhibit   “ A, ”   and be it further  RESOLVED ,   that   the   Township   Clerk   is   authorized   to   attest   to   the   Mayor's   signature;  and be it further

4822-4057-8218, v. 1  RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   thi s   resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk's   Office  for reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law .  K aren J.   Carnevale , R.M.C.   Jerry Guarino  Township Clerk   Council   President  Adopted:   Oc to ber 29, 2019

249-19  October 29, 2019  R E S O L U T I O N  BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, County of   Essex,  State   of New Jersey, that the contract   Fall 2019, Spring 2020 and Fall 2020 Tree Planting   with   a  W atering   O ption   in the Township of West Orange be awarded to:  LOUIS BA RBATO LANDSCAPING, INC.  1600 RAILROAD AVENUE  HOLBROOK, NEW YORK 11741  w ho submitted the lowest responsive bid for supplying 12 0   trees for Fall 2019 Planting in the amount of  $45,840.00; and  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED   that the option to plant 120 trees in Spri ng 2020 at a cost of  $46,320.00 and the second option to plant 120 trees in Fall 2020 at a cost of $52,560.00 be accepted  and a contract awarded for this work in 2020 subject to a budget appropriation in 2020, the  encumbrance of additional funds and the is suance of Purchase Orders.  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED   that certified checks and/or bid bonds of all except the lowest  responsive   bidder be forthwith returned   and the Mayor and Township Clerk be and they   hereby are  respectively authorized to execute and attest   a contract for the foregoing.  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED   that the bids for the water ing   option be rejected.  KAREN   J.   CARNEVALE,   R.M.C.,   MUNICIPAL CLERK   JERRY GUARINO, COUNCIL PRESIDENT  Ad opted: October 29, 2019  I   hereby certify funds are available from:   ACCOUNT NO.   2019 CAPITAL BUDGET  SHADE TREES  $46,320.00  JOHN GROSS, CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER

pages.text: 252-19  October 29, 2019  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   is   authorized   to   issue   certain   towing   licenses  for   a period of three (3) years ; and  WHEREAS ,   on   or   about   April   26,   2019 ,   the   Township   advertised   a   “ Notice   of   Public  Application   Process   for   Municipal   T owing   Contracts, ”   pursuant   to   an   application   process   under  N.J.S.A.   40A:11-5(1)(u) and Township Code Section 5-32; and  WHEREAS ,   full   and   complete   applications   with   all   required   responsive   materials   were  due from the applicants no l ater than   May 30, 2019   ( the   “ Application Deadline ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   t wo   ( 2 )   towing   companies   applied   for   towing   licenses   to   operate   in   the  Township   of   West   Orange,   including   Select   Towing   Service,   Inc.   ( “ Select ” ),   and   Twin,   Inc.   T/A  Twin Towing ( “ Twin Towing ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   pursuan t   to   Township   Code   Section   5-32.4(b),   after   the   Application  Deadline,   the   Township   Police   Department   reviewed   and   investigated   the   applicants   and  applications   to   determine   whether   the   requirements   of   all   applicable   Township   Code   Sec tions  were   fully   and   com pletely   satisfied ,   a   copy   of   the   Township   Police   Departments   Towing   License  Application Investigation (the   “ Investigation ” ) is annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A ” ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   I nvestigation   concluded   that   Select,   and   Twin   Towing   had   satisfied   the  objective   requirements of Township Code Section 5-32; and  WHEREAS ,   N.J.S.A.   40A:11-5(1)(u)   requires   that   all   awards   of   towing   contracts   be  made   on   “ non-exclusionary   and   non-discriminatory   terms   and   conditions, ”   which   terms   and  conditions   inc lude   satisfaction   of   the   Application   Deadline   and   satisfaction   of   the   requirements  of Township Code Section 5-32; and

2  4843-9194-8714, v. 1  WHEREAS ,   based   on   the   Investigation ,   the   Chief   of   Police   recommends   that   towing  operator licenses be issued to Select and Twin Towing .  N OW,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   TH E   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP  OF   WEST   ORANGE   that   towing   licenses   for   the   time   period   from   November   1 ,   201 9   through  October 31 , 20 22   be and are hereby awarded to Select and Twin Towing; and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Muni cipal   Clerk   shall   cause   a   copy   of   this   award   to   be   published  in accordance with applicable law ; and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Municipal   Clerk ’ s   Office   shall   maintain   a   copy   of   this   resolution  in its records.  Karen   J.   Carnevale , R.M. C.   Jerry Guarino  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   October 29, 2019  639091_1
Exhibit   “A”

253-19  October 29, 2019  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS,   the Township of West Orange has advertised for bids, pursuant to the New Jersey  Local   Public Contracts Law (N.J.S. A.   40A: 11-1) for the   2018 Road Improvements Phase III Meade  Street, Columbia Street, Tompkins Street and Stockman Street ;   and  WHEREAS,   at the date, time and place advertised for the opening of said bids, the Township did  recei ve   five   bids; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Engineer reported that the successful bidder has strictly complied  with the bid specifications and is the lowest responsible bidder; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council of the Township of West Orange has considered s aid bids  and has further considered the recommendation of the Township Engineer as to the award of said bid.  NOW, THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of West  Orange that the contract for the   2018 Road Improvements Phase III Me ade Street, Columbia Street,  Tompkins Street and Stockman Street awar ded to:  REGGIO CONSTRU C TION, INC.  1575 WEST STREET  FORT LEE, NEW JERSEY 07024  in the amount of $ 655,289 . 3 0.  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED   that Certified Checks and/or Bid Bonds of all   except the lowest  responsible bidder be forthwith returned, and the Mayor and Township Clerk be and they hereby are  respectively authorized to execute and attest a contract for the foregoing.  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED   that the award is contingent upon the Fe deral   clearance   of the  contractor   by the Essex County Division of Housing and Community Development.  Karen J. Route 1K-1 Lower Gregory will not be awarded at this  time, and will be retained and completed by   DPW personnel ; and  WHEREAS , hereto is attached   Schedule A   listing   the vendor s   with the  s ervices and rates that were bid .  NOW, THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by t he Township   Council of the  Township of West Orange   that a   one year service contract   with an option for up to  two additional one year periods   for Snow Plowing   Services for the   year(s) 201 9-2020  be awarded   to the   following   bidder   listed   on   Schedule A. 256-19  October 2 9, 2019  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the Township   Council by Resolution No. 129-19 dated April 30, 2019 awarded  Professional Services Contract to the firm Keller and Kirkpatrick, 301 Gibraltar Drive, Suite 2A, Morris  Plains, New Jersey 07950 in the amount of $28,300 for land surveying and engineering ser vices  associated with the Improvement of Dogwood Drive and Dogwood Road; and  WHEREAS,   during the design process the Municipal Engineer requested a change to the design  criteria at the intersection of Dogwood Drive and Old Short Hills Road to provide an im provement that  would prevent vehicles ,   except emergency   vehicles,   from turning left from Dogwood Drive onto Old  Short Hills Road southbound as that turn is prohibited by Ordinance; and  WHEREAS,   Keller and Kirkpatrick submitted a proposal dated   October 17, 2019 in the amount of  $2,900.00 to provide these additional design services; and  WHEREAS,   the Proposal is acceptable to the Municipal Engineer; and  WHEREAS,   sufficient funds exist for this additional work; and  WHEREAS,   The Township Council c oncurs in the foregoing.  NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange  that the Professional Services Contract with Keller and Kirkpatrick, 301 Gibraltar Drive, Suite 2A, Morris  Plains, New Jersey 07950 for the Improv ement of Dogwood Drive and Dogwood Road be amended in  accordance with their proposal dated October 17, 2019 for additional designs services for a fee of  $2,900.00.

BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED   that a copy of this Resolution be published in the West Orange  Chron icle within 10 days of its approval as required by State Statute.  KAREN   J.   CARNEVALE,   R.M.C.,   MUNICIPAL CLERK   JERRY GUARINO, COUNCIL PRESIDENT  Adopted: October 29, 2019  I hereby certify funds are available from:   ACCOUNT N O.  2018 Capital Budget  2019 Capital Budget  Street Resurfacing  JOHN GROSS, CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER

25 7 -19  R E S O L   U T I O N  WHEREAS,   the Township of West Orange by Purchase Order No. 159719 dated February 23,  2018 issued a Professional Services Contract to the firm Keller and Kirkpatrick, 301 Gibraltar Drive, Suite  2A, Morris Plains, New Jersey 07950 in the amount   of $17,430.00 for lan d surveying and engineering  services   associated with the project Street Improvements, 2018, Phase IIA; and  WHEREAS,   during the design process the Municipal Engineer requested a change to the Scope  of Services to include three addition al streets,   Lindsley   A venue   (Brennan Drive to Longview Street)  Condit Terrace (Brennan Drive to Longview Street) and Hutton Avenue from Northfield Avenue to  Randolph Place to supplement the improvement of two other streets in their neighborhood, Longview  S treet and Randolph Pl ace; and  WHEREAS,   Keller and Kirkpatrick submitted a proposal dated October 17, 2019 in the amount of  $3,900.00 to provide these additional design services; and  WHEREAS,   the Proposal is acceptable to the Municipal Engineer; and  WHER EAS,   sufficient funds   exist for this additional work; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council concurs in the foregoing,  NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   that the Township Council of the Township of West Orange  that the Professional Services Contract with Keller an d Kirkpatrick, 301 Gi braltar Drive, Suite 2A, Morris  Plains, New Jersey 07950 for Street Improvements 2018, Phase IIA be amended in accordance with their  proposal dated October 17, 2019 for additional designs   services for a fee of $3,900.00.

pages.text: 258-19  October 29, 2019  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   has   been   involved   in   certain   Affordable   Housing   Litigation  captioned   I n   the   M atter   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange ,   Docket   No.   ESX-L-4697-15   which   has  been   pending   before   the   Superior   Court   of   New   Jersey,   Law   Division,   Essex   County   for   over  four (4)   years; and  WHEREAS,   that   in   the   Affordable   Housing   Litigation,   Beth   McManus   has   been  designated   Special   Master   and   Ms.   McManus   has   been   attemptin g   to   amicably   resolve   various  issues; and  WHEREAS ,   West   Essex   Highlands ,   Inc.   has   intervened   in   the   Affordable   Housing  Litigation   and   has   asserted   that   its   tract   of   approximately   120   acres   of   vacant   land   is   available   to  meet   regional and local residential   needs   for affordable housing ; and  WHEREAS,   after   numerous   settlement   discussions   and   negotiations,   including  involvement   by   the   Highlands   Condominium   Association   through   its   president   and   counsel,   a  tentative settlement is in the midst of being finalized ; and  WHEREAS,   the   settlement   will   provide   for   a   substantial   housing   development   which  will have an impact on the   Township ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   is   concerned   regarding   the   development   process   and  especially the removal of trees   and drainage issues ; an d  WHEREAS,   the   Township   desire s   to   ensure   that   the   nearby   residents   are   fully   informed  as   to   all   aspects   of   development   including   staging,   blasting,   tree   removal   and   related   aspects   of  construction; and

4839-2899-4468, v. 1  WHEREAS,   Wayne   DeFeo   currently   serves   as   Environmenta l   Compliance   Officer   and  is   a   former   member   of   the   Board   of   Public   Utilities   and   teaches   at   Rutgers   University   in   the   areas  of recycling and environmental compliance; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   desires   that   this   potential   project   be   as   “ Green ”   and  environme ntally sensitive as possible; and  WHEREAS,   Amy   Green e   has   previously   studied   the   site   and   prepared   certain   reports   on  behalf of WE   CARE, a coalition of residents   from   the Highlands   Condominiums ; and  WHEREAS ,   this   a ward   i s   for   a   p rofessional   s ervices   c ontract   and   due   its   timing   and  exigencies   not   awarded   in   accordance   with   the   Fair   and   Open   Processes   set   forth   in   the   New  Jersey Statutes Annotated .  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   by   the   Township   Council   of   the  Township   of   West   Orange   tha t   Mayor   Parisi   is   hereby   authorized   to   enter   into   the   annexed  Professional   Services   Contract   and   the   Municipal   Clerk   is   authorized   to   attest   to   Mayor   Parisi ’ s  signature   with   Wayne   DeFeo   Associates   in   order   to   assist   in   the   initial   phase   of   reviewing   and  an alyzing   the   project   so   that   any   potential   settlement   will   incorporate   as   many   environmental  protections   as   possible   to   ensure   tha t   the   project   is   developed   in   an   environmentally   sensitive   and  comprehensive   manner   not to exceed $33,500 ; and  IT   IS   FURTHER   R ESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   Award   shall   be   available   in   the  Municipal Clerk ’ s office and advertised in accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. Carnevale,   R.M.C.   Jerry Guarino  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted: October 29,   2019

4837-3338-6922, v. 1  259-19  Oc tober 29, 2019  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   has   acquired   property  known   as   the   Rock   Spring   Golf   Course   including   the   various   facilities   and   operations   ( “ Rock  Spring ” ); and  WHEREAS,   Kemper Sports Management, I nc. ( “ Kemper ” ) operates Rock Spring; and  WHEREAS,   Kemper   has   entered   into   a   lease   agreement   with   TCF   Equipment   Finance  ( “ TCF ” )   for   seventy-two   (72)   pre-owned   2017   E-Z-Go   RXV   Elite   Golf   Carts   (the   “ Equipment ” )  which are leased by Kemper for operations at Ro ck Spring; and  WHEREAS,   TCF   and   Kemper   have   requested   the   Township   to   execute   the   agreement  with TCF, annexed hereto as Exhib it   “ A ” , relating to the leasing of the Equipment.  NOW   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORA NGE,   that   the   Township   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   execute   the  agreement with TCF; and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Mayo r   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   execute   any   agreement   with  TCF consistent with the proposed agreement annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A ; ”   and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office  in accordance w ith applicable law.  Karen Carnevale , R.M.C.   Jerry Guarino  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   October 29, 2019

2588-19  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING CHAPTER 7, TRAFFIC, SUBSECTION 7-13,  PARKING PROHIBITED AT ALL TIMES ON CERTAIN STREETS, OF THE REVISED GENERAL ORDINANCES  OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  BE IT ORDAINED,   by the Township Council of the Township of W est Orange, in the County of Essex, State  of New Jersey as follows:  Section 1.   Subsection   7-13 ,   Parking Prohibited at All Times   on Certain Streets,   of the Township of  West Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as follows: ( italic type denotes deletion ,   bold type denotes  addition):  Name of Street   Sides   Location  Forest Avenue   West   Beginning   3 5 feet north   of the   nor therly  curbline of Steven Terrace   and extending  sou therly   to a point   7 5 feet south of the  southerly cur bline of McManus Court.  Section 2.   If any provisions of this Ordinance or application thereof , under any circumstances, is  held invalid, the invalidity shall not affect any of the provisions or applications of this Ordinance that can be given  effect without the valid provision(s) or application and to this end, the provisions of this Ordinance are severable.  Section 3.   All other Ordinances or parts of Ordinances inconsistent with this Ordinance are hereby  repealed.  Section 4.   This Ordinance s hall take effect upon final passage and publication   in accordance with  law .  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Robert D. Parisi, Mayor  Municipal Clerk  Jerry Guarino,   Co uncil Presi dent  Introduced:   October 7 , 2019  Adopted:   October 29 , 2019

2588-19  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance amends the Revised General Ordinances of the Township of West Orange  Chapter 7-13, Parking Prohibited at All Times on Certain Streets , to add No   Parking on the west side of  Forest Avenue in the area of the McManus Court intersection. This change was requested by residents  of the Forest Creek development, who wanted improved sightlines for turning from McManus Court and  entering traffic on Forest A venue.

2589-19  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING CHAPTER 7, TRAFFIC,   SUBSECTION 7-32.1  HANDICAPPED PARKING ON STREETS AND   SUBSECTION 7-32.2,  RESTRICTED PARKING ZONES, OF THE REVISED GENERAL ORDINANCES  OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  BE IT ORDAINED,   by the Township Council   of the Township of West Orange, in the County of  Essex, State of New Jersey as follows:  Section 1.   Subsection 7-32.1, Handicapped Parking on Streets, of the Revised General  Ordinances of the Township   of   West Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as follows ( italic  type denotes deletion,   bold type denotes addition):  In accordance with the   provisions of N.J.S.A. 39:4-197, the following on-street locations are  designated as handicapped parking places. Such spaces are for use by persons who have been issued  special identification cards or plates or placards by the New Jersey Motor Vehicle Comm ission, or a  temporary placard issued by the Chief of Police. No other person shall be permitted to park in these  places.  Street   Side   No. of Spaces   Location  Valley Road   South east   1   Beginning 316   feet we st  of the   westerly curbline of  Dean Street   extended, and  extending   22   feet  south we st erly .  Section 2.   Subsection 7-32.2, Restricted Parking Zones, of the Revised General Ordinances  of the Township of West Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as follows ( italic type denotes  deletion,   bold type denotes addition):  7-32.2   Restricted Parking Zones. Pursuant to N.J.S.A 39:4-197.6, Restricted Parking Zones, as  defined in N.J.S.A. 39:4-207.6e, are established in front of the following residences as indicated on the  Tax Maps of the Township of West Orange, at the specified locat ions. Parking within a specific zone is  restricted to the motor vehicle issued a permit pursuant to N.J.S.A. 39:4-197.7. In accordance with  N.J.S.A., 39:4-197.6 parking is permitted in the Restricted Parking Zone except when prohibited in front  of the re sidence by other sections of this Chapter.

Section 2.   If any provision of this Ordinance or application thereof, under any  circumstances, is held invalid, the invalidity shall not af fect any other provisions or applications  of this Ordinance that can be given effect without the invalid provision(s) or application(s) and  to this end the provisions of this Ordinance are severable.  Section 3.   All other Ordinances or parts of   Ordinances inconsistent with this Ordinance are  hereby repealed.  Section 4.   This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication in  accordance with law and upon the approval of the New Jersey Department of Transportation if required.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Robert D. Parisi, Mayor  Municipal Clerk  Jerry Guarino,   Co uncil Presi dent  Introduced:   October 29,   2019  Adopted:   Nov ember 1 2, 2019

2589-19  L EGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordi nance is in response to   request s   from   handicappe d residen t s   who   reside   at   131 Watson  Avenue, 32 Edisonia Terrace, 205 Wat chung Avenue,   83 Lindsley   Avenue, 17 Dean Street, and   135 Main  Street . 72 Rollinson Street has been corrected to conform with superseding parking regulations .   A  handicapped parking space has been added for the use of disabled customers of a medical supply  business located at 480 Valley Road.  By adopting this Ordinance, the Township will establish a handicapped par king stall adjacent to  each of   these   address es .   Such parking stalls are permissible under the laws of New Jersey, and they are  designated as Restricted Parking Zones.   Additionally, two handicapped space no longer used have been  deleted from th e list .

pages.text: 241 - 19  October 7, 2019  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the Township of West Orange is the owner of a property located at   18  Central Avenue,   Block 9, Lot 36 on the Township of West Orange tax map   (the “ Property”) ; and  WHEREAS,   PSE&G has requested that the Township gra nt an easement through   the  Property for the installation of underground electrical facilities in order to provide service to a  neighboring property as depicted in the drawing annexed hereto as Exhibit   “A;”   and  WHEREAS,   the Township has prepared a proposed   Grant of Easement Agree ment to  grant PSE&G an easement through the Property for the purpose of installation and maintenance  of underground electrical facilities, annexed hereto as Exhibit   “B.”  NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED BY THE TOWNSHIP COUNCIL OF  THE T OWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE,   that the   Township   be and hereby is authorized to  execute the   Grant of Easement Agreement   annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ B ” ;   and be it further  RESOLVED,   that the Mayor is authorized to execute the   Grant of Easement Agreement  annexed her eto as Exhibit   “B” ; and   be it further  RESOLVED,   that the Township Clerk is authorized to witness the Mayor’s signature on  the   Grant of Easement Agreement ; and be it further  RESOLVED,   that a copy of this Resolution shall be made available in the Clerk’s   office  in accordance wi th applicable law.  Karen   J.   Carnevale , R.M.C.   Jerry Guari no  Muni ci pal   Clerk   Council President  A dopted:   O ctober 7, 2019
Exhibit   “A”
Exhibit   “B”

Record and Return to:  PSEG Services Corporation  Corporate Properties Dept  80 Park Plaza, t6b  Newark, New Jersey 07102  GRANT OF EASEMENT  THIS   INDENTURE,   made   this   _____   day   of   ____________,   2019,   between  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE , with an office at 66 Main Street, West Orange, NJ 07052, (hereinafter called “Grantor”), and   PUBLIC SERVICE ELECTRIC AND GAS COMPANY , a corporation of the State of New Jersey, having its office at 80 Park Plaza, Newark, New Jersey 07102 (hereinafter called “Grantee”).  WHEREAS,   Grantor is the owner in fee simple of a certain tract of real property situate in the Township of West Orange County of Essex and State of New Jersey, commonly known as Block 9, Lot 36 (hereinafter the “Property”); and  WHEREAS,   Grantee is a public utility of the State of New Jersey, engaged in furnishing utility service to subscribers in the State of New Jersey; and  WHEREAS,   the Grantor does agree to convey an easement in perpetuity to Grantee for its use, occupancy and enjoyment and the use, occupancy and enjoyment of its licensees, successors in interest and assigns, in connection with the provision of utility service which crosses its Property for service to its neighboring property known as Block 9 Lot 1.01 in the Township of West Orange County of Essex and State of New Jersey and for the conduct of its business, all in accordance with and for the purposes set forth in this Grant of Easement, for the mutual benefit of both Grantor and Grantee;  NOW THEREFORE, WITNESSETH:   In consideration of these premises and the sum of ONE ($1.00) DOLLAR, paid to the Grantor by the Grantee, the receipt of which is hereby acknowledged, and in further consideration of the mutual conditions, covenants, promises and terms hereinafter contained, it is agreed that:  1.   Grantor does hereby grant and convey unto Grantee an easement in perpetuity, in, under, through, upon, over and across the hereinbefore described Property of Grantor, with full rights, privileges and authority for Grantee to enter upon same from time to time, for the purpose of   inspecting,   locating,   relocating,   installing,   altering,   extending,   constructing,   repairing, replacing, rebuilding, removing and perpetually operating, maintaining and using underground electrical facilities and other fixtures, including but not limited to wires and cables, guy wires, anchors,   conduits   and   manholes   (hereinafter   the   “Facilities”),   which   Grantee   may   deem necessary or proper in its sole judgment for the conduct of its business; together with such free and unlimited access to, egress and ingress in, from and over all points of said Property, as is reasonable or necessary for the full use, occupancy and enjoyment of said easement.   Said

easement area and the Facilities to be installed therein are more particularly shown on Drawing No. _____________ attached hereto and made a part hereof.  2.   Grantor does further grant and convey to Grantee the right, privilege and authority to trim, cut and remove such tree branches, roots, shrubs, plants, trees and vegetation which might, within the exclusive discretion and sole judgment of Grantee, interfere with or threaten the safe, proper or convenient use, maintenance or operation of the Facilities within the easement area. Grantee shall not be responsible for any damage to any trees or other vegetation due to the installation of the Facilities.  3.   Grantor shall have the right to use, occupy and enjoy the surface and air space around the easement area for any purpose that does not interfere or threaten the safe, proper or convenient use, occupancy or enjoyment of same by Grantee. Grantor agrees, however, that that no buildings or structures shall be erected over or within ten (10) feet of   the Facilities of Grantee except as is currently located on the Property.  4.   Grantor shall have the right to allow other utilities to use the said easement area for any purpose, which does not in any way interfere with the accessibility and safe operation of the Facilities of Grantee, and subject to the consent of Grantee.   Grantor’s right to allow other utilities to use the easement area does not include the right to allow other utilities to use the Facilities that Grantee has installed in the easement. Grantor’s right to allow other utilities to use the easement area shall in no way limit the rights granted to Grantee in this Easement.  5.   Grantee shall perform all work in connection with the rights, privileges and authority herein granted and conveyed in a workmanlike manner and with a minimum of inconvenience to the Grantor; and any damage done to the land or premises of Grantor shall be promptly repaired and restored to its condition immediately prior to damage, at the sole cost and expense of Grantee.  6.   If Grantor shall, at any time after the initial installation of the Facilities, request Grantee to relocate the Facilities to a different location or locations, it shall do so at such location or locations as shall be mutually satisfactory to the parties hereto, at the sole cost and expense of Grantor or the owner of Block 9, Lot 1.01, West Orange, Essex County, NJ, Grantee to have the same rights and privileges in the new location or locations as in the former location or locations.  7.   Grantor covenants to warrant generally the rights above granted, will execute such further assurance of the same as may be required, and that Grantee shall have the quiet possession thereof free from all encumbrances.  8.   Grantee shall defend and indemnify Grantor against, and shall save Grantor harmless from, and shall reimburse Grantor with respect to, any and all claims, demands, actions, causes of action, injuries, orders, losses, liabilities (statutory or otherwise), obligations, damages, fines, penalties, costs and expenses (including without limitation, reasonable attorneys’ fees and expenses) incurred by, imposed upon or asserted against Grantor by reason of any accident, injury (including death at any time resulting therefrom) or damage to any person or property

arising out of or resulting from any acts or omissions of Grantee or by any employee, licensee, invitee or agent of Grantee.  9.   This Grant of Easement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New Jersey and recorded on the title to the Property.  10.   By the acceptance if this instrument, Grantee agrees to abide by the terms and conditions herein on its part to be performed and shall be deemed signatory hereto, and the provisions of this indenture shall inure to the benefit of and be obligatory upon the respective parties hereto and their successors and assigns.  IN WITNESS WHEREOF,   Grantor has duly signed these presents the day and year first above written.  ATTEST:   GRANTOR:  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  By:_______________________   By:  Karen J. Carnevale   Robert D. Parisi, Mayor

STATE OF   )  : SS.  COUNTY OF   )  BE IT REMEMBERED,   that on this _____ day of ___________, 2019, before me, the subscriber, a Notary Public of the State of _________________________, personally appeared Robert D. Parisi who, I am satisfied, is Mayor of Township of West Orange, the municipal corporation named in and which executed the foregoing instrument and is the person who signed said instrument as such officer for and on behalf of said corporation and sealed with its corporate seal, as the voluntary act and deed of said corporation, by virtue of authority from its Board of Directors.   The full and actual consideration paid or to be paid for the transfer of title to realty evidenced by the within easement, as such consideration is defined in P.L. 1968, c. 49, Sec. 1(c), is less than $100.00.  ____________________________________ Notary

pages.text: 242 - 19  October 7, 2019  RESOLUTION   ESTABLISHING A LAND USE PLEDGE COMMITT ING TO GOOD,  PLANNED AND TH OUGHTFUL LAND USE DECISIONS WITH THE INTENT OF M AKING  THE TOWNSHIP OF   WEST ORANGE   A SUSTAINABLE COMMUNITY  WHEREAS,   the Townsh ip of West Or ange is committe d   to having a sustai nable future, and has enrolled  as a participant in the st ate - wi de Sustainable Jerse y program; and  WHEREAS,   the Township of West Orange prides itself on making informed, and beneficial land - us e  decisions to improve societ al well - being,   such as increased mobility, affordable hous ing, reduced  greenhouse gas em issions, protection of its open space and natural   resources; and  W HEREAS ,   the Township of We st Ora n ge recognizes that poor land use decisions can degrade the  environment, reduce hous ing and transportation choices , increase health costs, and reduce overall well -  being of its residents; and  W HEREAS,   the State of New Jersey has a str ong   traditi on of home rule and local authority over  pla nning   and   zoning , which requires that municip alitie s must take a leading role in achi eving statewide  susta inable land use patterns.  NOW, THEREFORE BE IT R ESOLVED ,   that the Township of West Oran ge is committed to  continuing   good, planned and thoughtful land use decisions with the intent of making our Towns hip a  sustainable community.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C. , Municipa l Clerk   Jer ry Guarino, Council President  Adopted: October 7, 2019

2588-19  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING CHAPTER 7, TRAFFIC, SUBSECTION 7-13,  PARKING PROHIBITED AT ALL TIMES ON CERTAIN STREETS, OF THE REVISED GENERAL ORDINANCES  OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  BE IT ORDAINED,   by the Township Council of the Township of W est Orange, in the County of Essex, State  of New Jersey as follows:  Section 1.   Subsection   7-13 ,   Parking Prohibited at All Times   on Certain Streets,   of the Township of  West Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as follows: ( italic type denotes deletion ,   bold type denotes  addition):  Name of Street   Sides   Location  Forest Avenue   West   Beginning   3 5 feet north   of the   nor therly  curbline of Steven Terrace   and extending  sou therly   to a point   7 5 feet south of the  southerly cur bline of McManus Court.  Section 2.   If any provisions of this Ordinance or application thereof , under any circumstances, is  held invalid, the invalidity shall not affect any of the provisions or applications of this Ordinance that can be given  effect without the valid provision(s) or application and to this end, the provisions of this Ordinance are severable.  Section 3.   All other Ordinances or parts of Ordinances inconsistent with this Ordinance are hereby  repealed.  Section 4.   This Ordinance s hall take effect upon final passage and publication   in accordance with  law .  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Robert D. Parisi, Mayor  Municipal Clerk  Jerry Guarino,   Co uncil Presi dent  Introduced:   October 7 , 2019  Adopted:   October 29 , 2019

2588-19  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance amends the Revised General Ordinances of the Township of West Orange  Chapter 7-13, Parking Prohibited at All Times on Certain Streets , to add No   Parking on the west side of  Forest Avenue in the area of the McManus Court intersection. This change was requested by residents  of the Forest Creek development, who wanted improved sightlines for turning from McManus Court and  entering traffic on Forest A venue.

pages.text: 229-19  September 24, 2019  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   acquired   through   an   In  Rem   Tax   Foreclosure,   the   Properties   located   at   18   Central   Avenue,   Block   9,   Lot   36   ( “ 18  Central ” ) ,   and   4   Tompkins   Street,   Block   7,   Lot   22   ( “ 4   Tompkins ” )   on   the   Tax   Map   of   the  Township of West Orange ( collectively   the   “ Properties ” ); and  WHEREAS,   on   August   9,   2016,   t he   Township   adopted   resolution   189-16   to   authorize  Matrix   New   World   ( “ Matrix ” )   to   apply   for   grant   funds   from   the   New   Jersey   Department   of  Envir onmental   Protection/New   Jersey   Economic   Development   Authority   (the   “ DEP ” )   Hazardous  Discharge   Site   Remediation   Funds   (the   “ HDSRF ” )   Program   for   the   purpose   of   funding   the   costs  for pe r forming the Preliminary Assessment and Site Investigation on the Properti es; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   received   an   award   of   $77,237.90   from   the   HDSRF   Program  to fund the Preliminary Assessment and Site Investigation on the Properties; and  WHEREAS,   on   March   7,   2017,   the   Township   adopted   resolution   75-17,   to   authorize  Matrix to p erform the Preliminary Assessment and Site Investigation on the Properties; and  WHEREAS,   on   June   25,   2019,   the   Township   adopted   resolution   170-19,   to   authorize  Matrix   to   complete   the   Remedial   Investigation   and   prepare   a   Remedial   Investigation   Report   and  a   Remedial   Action   Work   Plan   for   the   Properties   utilizing   $129,177   in   HDSRF   Grant   Funds  awarded by the DEP ; and  WHEREAS,   during   the   course   of   the   Remedial   Investigation   of   the   Properties,   Matrix  discovered   that   three   (3)   of   the   monitoring   wells   on   18   Central   (the   “ Monitoring   Wells ” )   were  damaged   and   required   repairs   or   replacement   to   allow   Matrix   to   complete   the   Remedial  Investigation and the Remedial Action Work Plan for the Properties ; and

2  4822-1398-5446, v. 1  WHEREAS,   Matrix   has   provided   an   estimate   that   the   costs   of   the   repairs   to   the  Monitoring Wells will be about $20,720; and  WHEREAS,   the   repairs   and   replaceme nts   of   the   Monitoring   Wells   will   require   the  submission   of:   (i)   an   application   to   the   DEP   to   reallocate   the   $129,177   in   HDSRF   grant   funds  previously   awarded   for   the   preparation   of   a   Remedial   Action   Work   Plan   to   move   funds   towards  the   repair   of   the   Monitoring   Wells;   and   (ii)   an   application   to   the   DEP   for   supplemental   HDSRF  Grant   funding   to   replace   the   funds   util ized   for   the   repairs   of   the   Monitoring   Wells   for   the   tasks  needed   to   complete   the   preparation   of   a   Remedial   Action   Work   Plan   (the   “ HDSRF  Applications ” ); and  WHEREAS,   Matrix   has   submitted   a   proposal   for   the   preparation   of   the   HDSRF  Applications to the DEP , annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A; ”   and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   the   proposal,   Matrix   will   prepare   the   HDSRF   Applications   for   a  cost not to exceed $5,500.  NOW, BE IT HEREBY RESOLVED BY THE TOWNSHIP COUNCIL OF THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   that   the   Township   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   retain  Matrix   to   prepare   the   HDSRF   Application   to   reallocate   and   seek   supplemental   funding   to   be  utilized for the repair of monitoring wells on 18 Central ;   and be it further  RESOLVED ,   that   the   Mayor   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   the   acceptance   of   the  P roposal annexed   hereto as Exhibit   “ A , ”   and be it   further  RESOLVED , that the Township Clerk is authorized to attest to the Mayor's signature;  and be it further

3  4822-1398-5446, v. 1  RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk's   Office  for reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law . Matrix New World En gineer ing, Land Surveying and  Landscape Architecture, P.C.  26 Columbia Turnpi ke  Florham Park, NJ 07932  973.240.1800 Fax 973.240.1818  www.matrixneworld.com   WBE/DB E/SBE  F: \ 2017 \ 17 - 296 Selecto Flash PA_SI \ HDSRF \ 2019 Reallocation \ Twp. of West Orange   -   HDSRF Supplemental Application_Reallocation_REV 1.doc  Via Em ail ( JSayers@westorange.org )  September   1 7 , 2019  Township of   West Orange  66 Main Street  West Orange, N ew   J ersey   07052  Attn:   John K. Sayers, Business Administrator  RE:   HDSRF GRANT APPL ICATION   –   REALLOCATION AND   SUPPLMENTAL FUNDING   REQUEST  TOWNSHI P   OF WEST ORANGE, ESS EX COUNTY, NEW JERSE Y  SELECTO FLASH INCORPORATED (FORMER)  18 CENTRAL AVENUE  BLOCK 9, LOT 36  SRP PI NO. 027054  MATRIX NO. 17 - 296  REVIS ION 1  Dear   Mr.   Sayers :  Matrix New World Engineering, Lan d Surveying and Landscape Architecture, P.C. (Ma t r ix)   has prepared this  proposal   for environmental services for   the   re allocation of grant fund s and the prepar ation of the   supplemental  Rem edial I nvestigation   Hazardous Discharge Site Remediation Fund (HDSRF)   grant application for   the Selecto Flash  property   located at 18 Central Avenue , W est Orange, New J e r sey .   This proposal has been revised to reflect the  September 17, 2019, communication between Richard Trenk and   Dennis   Petrocelli .  SCOPE OF   WORK  The scope   of services and associated fee   is   described in detail below:  TASK   1:   RE ALLOCATION OF   FUND S FROM   EXISTING   HDS RF   RI   GRANT  In June 2018 an application for H DSRF funds to   complete th e RI for the   Selecto Flash Site was submi tted to NJDEP .  The total of this   HDSRF RI Grant was $129,176.80.   The   original   SOW for the RI included the installation of up to  five shallow overburden monitoring wells (MW - 7, 8, 9,   10, and 11 installe d   and sample d   in 2019) and three vertical  delineation   (deep) monitoring wells (MW - 4D, MW - 9D, and MW - 11D installed   and sampled   in   2019).   Matrix   visited  the   Site on   August 9, 2019 in order t o   l ocate   original   monitoring wells MW - 1, MW - 2, MW - 3, and MW - 5, and  determine i f the y   were in condition to be sampled or   would need to be reinstalled or rehabilitated . Monitoring well  MW - 3 has not yet been located ,   MW - 5 has   been   destroyed, and MW - 1   and MW - 2 , require reh abilitation.   As the cost  for the rep lacement and/or   rehabilitat ion of the original monitoring wells   would be in addition to the   HDSRF   funding  amount that remains,   funds   w ill be   reallocated for   the   completion of this wor k .  Matrix   has discussed the reallocation with John Doyon of NJDEP HDSRF and will prep are the requ est   for  reallocation   o f   HDSRF   funds in acco rdance with his requirements. The reallocation will   allow monies to be shifted  for the   repair and/or   replacement   of   the original   m onitoring wells   and will   include   a spreadsheet showing   the original  grant funds , what   tasks   are being   reallocate d ,   and what new tasks are   being   added .   T he   reallocation request  appl ication package wi ll be submitted electroni cally to John Doyon , HDSR F/BDA   Coordinator .   This   task   also  includes   coordination with   the Clien t , the Clien t ’ s attorney, the Designat ed Redeveloper, and NJDEP   .  The   amount of the original   H DSRF Grant for   the RI and the reallocated totals will be the same: $129,176.80.   The  tasks that were removed from   the SOW   to allow for the well rehabilitation a nd/or r eplac ement   ( totaling   approximately  $21,000)   will be paid for   with a   S upplemental HDSRF Grant to complete   the RI   (Task 2) .

F: \ 2017 \ 17 - 296 Selecto Flash PA_SI \ HDSRF \ 2019 Reallocation \ Twp. of West Orange   -   HDSRF Supplemental Applicati on_Reallocation_REV 1.doc  2  TASK 2 :   PREPARATION   OF   SUPPLEMENTAL   R EM EDIAL   INVESTIGATION   HDS RF   GRANT APPLICA TION  In order to reallocate funds for the   well rehabilitation and/or r eplacemen t , a number of tasks to be completed as part of  the original SOW will   be moved into a Supplemental HDSRF Grant to   complete   the RI.   These tasks include the  completion of th e   RI Report (RIR), the Rem edial Action Workpl an (RAW), the completion of   Vapor I ntrusion   (VI)  Sampling , and   some well abandonment costs.   An additional (contingent )   round of soil and groundwater sampling will  also be added to the supplemental grant.   T ask   2   includes   preparation of required grant appli cation forms,   and  coordination with the   Client , the Client ’ s attorney, the Designat ed Redeveloper, and NJDEP   in order to complete  Sections A through   E of Part I of the H DSRF application , s cope of work ,   cost est imate ,   Part I I   –   Ge neral Certification  of the   HDSRF application ,   Part III   –   Subrog ation of Rights of the HDSRF application ,   and   Part VI   –   Municipal  Questionnaire of the HDSRF application .  C OSTS  The following are our   fixed fee   costs for the tasks described   herein.   All   work will be conducted in accor dance  with the   previously transmitted   standard terms and conditions and LSR P Special P rovisions.  TASK   1 :   RE ALLOCATION OF   FUND S FROM   EXISTING   HDS RF   RI   GRANT  Lab or   ................................ ................................ ................................ ................................ ..........................   $ 1 , 490 . 0 0  Expen ses   ................................ ................................ ................................ ................................ .........................   $ 40 .00  F ixed Fee C ost   ................................ ................................ ..........   $ 1 , 5 3 0 . 0 0  TASK   2 :   PREPARATION   OF   SUPPLEMENTAL   R E M EDIAL   INVESTIGATION   HDS RF   GRANT APPLICA TION  Labor   ................................ ................................ ................................ ................................ ..........................   $ 3,850 . 0 0  Expenses   ................................ ................................ ................................ ................................ .......................   $ 1 20 .00  F ixed Fee C ost   ................................ ................................ ..........   $ 3,970 . 0 0  Total   F ixed Fee C ost   ................................ ................................   $ 5 ,5 0 0 . 0 0  CLOSING  Separate authorization from the cli ent wil l be requeste d prior   to commencing   additional   well installations,  a ban donments, or   services outside the scope of this proposal.  All out - of - pocket expenses   inclu ding, but not limited to, applicat ion f ees, laborat ory testin g costs, mylar copies,  certified mailings, photographs,   blueprints,   and spe cial de liveries are   consid ered additional to the proposal items  unless specifically noted within the scope of this   propo sal.  This proposal is submi tted s olely   and exclusi vely for t he use of   Township of West Orange   for consideration of   the  pro fessi onal ser vices o f Matrix.   Disclosu re of this proposal’s content to any third party without prior written  authorization from Matr ix is expressly prohibited.  In ad ditio n to the spe cific item s as listed herein, the client may be required to demon strate   compl iance wi th cert ain  permit and appr oval conditions as may be imposed by one or more of the regulatory agencies. These conditions   may  require revisions to th e plan s and /or preparat ion of add itional supporting documentation.   This proposal does n ot  incl ude t hese add itional   items unless speci fically outlined within the scope of this proposal.
